questions.text: Tell me about yourself and walk me through your resume.

recommendations: Revise essential HR concepts on our Study Roadmap.

flashcards.back: Encapsulation binds data (variables) and code (methods) together into a single unit, shielding it from external access.

flashcards.front: What is an Index in SQL?
flashcards.back: A database index is a data structure (like B-Tree) that improves data retrieval speeds, similar to a book index.

questions.question: Tell me about yourself and walk me through your resume.
Why do you want to work at Amazon, and how do you resonate with our Customer Obsession leadership principle?
Tell me about a time when you had a severe conflict with a manager or teammate. How did you handle it and what did you learn?
Why should we hire you, and are you comfortable relocating to different project sites if required?
How do you handle working under tight deadlines and managing high-pressure situations?
Explain the four main pillars of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) with real-world examples.
What is the difference between Virtual DOM and Shadow DOM? How does React optimize rendering using the Virtual DOM?
How do indexes work internally in databases? Explain the differences between B-Trees and Hash indexes and when to use each.
What are the differences between INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN in SQL? Give a practical scenario.
What is overfitting in Deep Learning? How do you detect it, and what regularization techniques can prevent it?
Describe a project that failed despite your best efforts. What went wrong, and how did you communicate the failure to stakeholders?
Give me an example of a time when you had to make a critical decision with extremely limited data or time constraints. What was the outcome?

questions.title: Reverse a String
questions.description: Write a function `reverseString(s)` that takes a string and returns its reversed version.

questions.title: Two Sum
questions.description: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution.

questions.title: Palindrome Number
questions.description: Determine whether an integer `x` is a palindrome. Return `true` if it is, and `false` otherwise.

templates.python:
def is_palindrome(x):
    # Write your code here
    if x < 0: return False
    return str(x) == str(x)[::-1]
